title: Hedge
author: Jane Delury
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Hedge" book by Jane Delury?
Options:
A. She informed me that she could return tomorrow. Alice recommended keeping to Wednesdays. The subsequent week, Maud toiled quicker and more diligently, attempting to complete the center of the field, laying out the thick plastic sheets. Alice remarked that she had spotted a whale, walking past as Maud hammered in the stakes. Maud asked if there was an ocean view. Alice affirmed there was a spectacular ocean view. Alice offered lunch to Maud when she finished. Maud accepted. She had ample time before collecting Ella and Louise. Alice mentioned she was going to the library to read. The table would be set at twelve o'clock.
B. I can come back tomorrow, she said. Lets stick to Wednesdays, Alice said. The following week, Maud worked faster and harder, trying to get the middle of the field done, rolling out the thick sheets of plastic. Spotted a whale, Alice said, passing by as Maud hammered in stakes. Theres a view of the ocean? Maud said. Theres a hell of a view of the ocean. You want lunch when youre done? Sure, Maud said. She had plenty of time before she needed to pick up Ella and Louise. Im going to the library to read, Alice said. The table will be set at twelve.
C. She said she could come back the following day. Let's adhere to Wednesdays, Alice proposed. The next week, Maud labored faster and harder, striving to finish the middle of the field, unrolling the thick plastic sheets. I saw a whale, mentioned Alice, passing as Maud drove in the stakes. Is there a view of the ocean? inquired Maud. There's a hell of an ocean view, replied Alice. Do you want lunch when you finish? Sure, responded Maud. She had plenty of time before getting Ella and Louise. I'm going to the library to read, stated Alice. Lunch will be on the table at twelve.  
D. She told me she could return the next day. Alice suggested they keep to Wednesdays. The next week, Maud worked quicker and more intensely, attempting to complete the middle of the field, laying out the thick plastic sheets. Alice said she saw a whale, walking by as Maud hammered in the stakes. Maud asked if there was an ocean view. Alice said there was a fantastic ocean view. Alice asked if Maud wanted lunch when finished. Maud agreed. She had ample time before picking up Ella and Louise. Alice mentioned she was going to the library to read. The table would be set at twelve.
Answer:
